creator: chuck@FreeBSD.org
text:
The approach I've been using is let the VM boot, attach a debugger to the bhyve process and then do the thing which causes the core dump (in your case, a reboot in/of the VM). E.g.

$ sudo vm start u18-unh-00
Starting u18-unh-00
  * found guest in /vms/u18-unh-00
  * booting...
$ ps waux|grep bhyve
root    3430   33.3  0.0 4247640 29376  0  SC   18:23    0:02.56 bhyve: u18-unh-00 (bhyve)
ctuffli 3441    0.0  0.0   12904  2616  0  S+   18:23    0:00.00 grep bhyve
$ sudo lldb
(lldb) attach -p 3430
Process 3430 stopped
